articles.title: IMD forecasts heatwave conditions in Uttarakhand from May 25 to 27 - The News Mill
articles.full_text:
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) in Dehradun has forecast the possibility of heatwave-like conditions in the plain districts of Uttarakhand from May 25 to 27. Specifically, certain areas in Haridwar, Udham Singh Nagar, Dehradun, Pauri, Nainital, and Champawat districts are expected to encounter these conditions.
The State Emergency Operations Centre has issued an advisory instructing all districts to remain alert and to implement necessary precautionary measures. District Emergency Operation Centres and control rooms have been directed to operate continuously.
Authorities have been asked to coordinate with the Health Department to ensure the adequate availability of medicines, Oral Rehydration Salts (ORS), ice, and essential medical equipment in hospitals. Special arrangements are to be made for treating patients affected by heatwaves and dehydration.
Urban local bodies have been ordered to guarantee the provision of drinking water, water kiosks, and cooling water facilities at public locations including markets, bus stands, railway stations, and major intersections. The advisory also recommends setting up temporary shelters and rest areas in crowded places.
The Power and Drinking Water Departments have been instructed to maintain uninterrupted supply and to organise water tankers in areas experiencing water shortages. Workers and personnel at outdoor worksites are advised to limit working hours to the morning and evening periods.
Vinod Kumar Suman, Secretary of Disaster Management and Rehabilitation, has urged the public to avoid unnecessary outdoor movement during peak afternoon heat and to consume sufficient amounts of water and other fluids to stay hydrated.
